text: meta-Nitroaniline (mNA, 3-nitroaniline), chemical formula C6H6N2O2, molecular weight 138.12, is a single-component polar organic molecular crystal widely studied as a nonlinear-optical material. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, polar orthorhombic space group Pbc2_1 (No. 29, point group mm2), with unit cell a = 6.501 Å, b = 19.330 Å, c = 5.082 Å, Z = 4, and the polar axis along c. Because the mm2 point group is polar, mNA is piezoelectric and pyroelectric-compatible. In this work, three piezoelectric coefficients of mNA single crystals were measured at room temperature by synchrotron X-ray multiple diffraction, monitoring field-induced lattice strain via the converse piezoelectric effect: the transverse |d31| = 7.3 x 10^-11 m/V (about 73 pm/V), |d32| = 16.5 x 10^-11 m/V (about 165 pm/V, confirmed as 14.9 x 10^-11 m/V in a second reflection), and the longitudinal |d33| = 10.3 x 10^-11 m/V (about 103 pm/V); strain varied linearly and reversibly with applied field. The authors note these inverse-effect values run larger than direct-effect literature values and are presented as a feasibility demonstration rather than precise determinations. The crystal structure (CCDC 1212672, CSD refcode MNIANL10) was determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction at 295 K with an R factor of 8.4% and a density of 1.437 g/cm3, reported by Skapski and Stevenson (1973). The piezoelectric-coefficient measurement was reported by Avanci et al. in Physical Review Letters (1998).
structure_ref_journal: Journal of the Chemical Society, Perkin Transactions 2
property_ref_title: Piezoelectric Coefficients of mNA Organic Nonlinear Optical Material Using Synchrotron X-Ray Multiple Diffraction
